summary: Matched a large block of game-mode code centered on src/melee/gm/gm_16AE.c, replacing many M2C stubs and UNK prototypes with typed C implementations and supporting header/type fixes. The decomp.dev report credited 38 new matches and +5380 matched bytes, with the largest new gm_16AE matches including fn_8016C0C8, fn_8016BC74, fn_8016B918, fn_8016BF74, fn_8016B5B0, fn_8016C35C, gm_8016BE80, and related stock/pad/stadium/game-speed helpers. The PR also matched gm_801B089C in gm_1B03.c and improved partially unmatched gm_80173EEC, fn_8016CA68, and gm_8016B8D4.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_16AE functions around match rules, mode predicates, game speed, stage/stadium transitions, stock sharing, pause/input checks, and match-end/control flow.

Updated gm_1601.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for the newly matched gm_16AE range, including bool/int/void/float return types and concrete int, s16, UNK_T, pointer parameters.
Replaced several raw M2C_FIELD accesses into lbl_8046B6A0 with named struct fields such as unk_6, unk_B, unk_18, unk_2E, unk_38[arg0].x6, and unk_24C.
Refined struct lbl_8046B6A0_t layout in gm/types.h: changed signedness/types for several fields, split padding around 0x2E/0x30, added unk_24C, and corrected padding boundaries.
Extended StartMeleeRules in mn/types.h by documenting x0_0 as a likely match-mode bitfield and replacing padding at x3C/x40 with function pointers.
Added a substantial ifstock.h prototype surface, including ifStock_802F7EFC, so gm_16AE stock-sharing code could call interface stock helpers with a declared signature.
Adjusted lbTime_8000AF24 from a three-argument to a two-argument function and moved the third variable into a local int, matching its observed use in gm_8016B6E8.
Matched gm_801B089C(MinorScene*) in gm_1B03.c, using gm_801A4284 input flags, debug-level branches, gm_80173EEC, gm_80172898, gm_80173754, gm_801BF708, and gm_801A42E8 scene transitions.
Changed supporting prototypes in gm_17C0.h, gmmain_lib.h, lbaudio_ax.h, and un_2FC9.h where concrete argument/return types were needed by the matched code.
The bot report recorded matched code rising to 33.94% (+0.14%, +5380 bytes), 38 new matches, and 3 improvements in still-unmatched functions.
smart_moves: Recovered the shared global game-mode state layout enough to remove brittle M2C_FIELD expressions and write direct field accesses, which made multiple nearby functions match together.
Used small static inline helpers to control codegen and share repeated logic, including fn_8016B918_inline for stock-share teammate selection and gm_8016B41C_dontinline for call-shape preservation.
Kept explicit PAD_STACK calls in functions with sensitive stack layout, e.g. gm_8016B014, fn_8016B5B0, fn_8016B918, fn_8016BC74, gm_8016BE80, and later gm_16AE logic.
Converted broad UNK prototypes into exact bool/int/float/void signatures across headers, enabling cleaner callers and reducing casts while preserving matching.
Matched stock-sharing behavior by modeling the exact controller-input/debug-level split: debug builds check trigger 8 plus button 0x400, while normal builds use trigger 0x1000.
Localized a library signature correction in lbTime_8000AF24 instead of papering over the call site, making later time-bitfield callers more likely to match.
Implemented gm_SetGameSpeed and gm_ResetGameSpeed directly around lb_80019880 and OS_TIMER_CLOCK, exposing the intended 1/60-speed timing formula rather than leaving opaque arithmetic.
decomp_lessons: For gm_16AE-style global-state code, invest in the lbl_8046B6A0_t struct layout first; named fields can unlock many small predicate/accessor matches and eliminate M2C_FIELD noise.
Header prototypes are part of matching work: changing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to exact signatures can be required for caller codegen, not just readability.
When a function appears to take an unused extra parameter, verify whether it is actually a local variable in the callee; lbTime_8000AF24 matched after the former third parameter became a local int.

Bitfield and callback recovery in StartMeleeRules matters for gameplay rule helpers; x0_0 was annotated as likely match mode, and x3C/x40 were recovered as function pointers instead of anonymous padding.
Stock-share and controller-check code should model both debug and retail input paths because g_debugLevel-dependent branches affect matching shape.
Small inline wrappers can be useful matching tools when the source-level logic is clear but the compiler needs a specific inlining or non-inlining boundary.
Use existing subsystem helpers once their headers are declared: gm_16AE matching depended on calls into Player, Ground, grStadium, ifStatus, ifStock, ftLib, Stage, un, lbTime, and gm scene libraries.
naming_conventions: Unidentified game-mode functions retain address-based names such as gm_8016AF0C, gm_8016B014, fn_8016B918, and fn_8016C0C8.
Public or clearer helpers use semantic names where already established, e.g. gm_SetGameSpeed and gm_ResetGameSpeed.
Global-state struct fields remain placeholder-style but offset-oriented, e.g. lbl_8046B6A0_t::unk_2E, unk_38[index].x6, and unk_24C.

Recovered function signatures use concrete C types in headers where known, preferring bool for predicate helpers, int for controller/player IDs or status codes, float for scalar gameplay parameters, and void for side-effect-only routines.
Address comments in headers are preserved beside prototypes, e.g. /* 16B918 */ void fn_8016B918(void);.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK with exact byte counts to satisfy stack layout in several newly matched functions.
Used #pragma dont_inline around existing sensitive code and added a separate gm_8016B41C_dontinline inline wrapper to influence call/inlining shape.
Replaced pointer arithmetic and M2C_FIELD macros with struct field accesses after updating gm/types.h layout.
Preserved sentinel values and loop bounds visible in matched code, such as iterating six player slots and using 6 as the no-teammate sentinel in fn_8016B918_inline.
Matched switch-based mode predicates over gm_801A4310 return values instead of collapsing them into more abstract condition tables.
Maintained explicit temporary variables and bool temporaries around HSD_PadStatus checks, likely to preserve branch/codegen structure.
Used float constants and arithmetic as source-level evidence required, e.g. 99.0F / 59.0F for gm_8016AF0C and 1 / 60.0F / speed * OS_TIMER_CLOCK for game speed.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported 38 new matches, +5380 matched bytes, and matched code increasing to 33.94% (+0.14%).
The bot also reported improvements, not full matches, for gm_80173EEC at 84.24%, fn_8016CA68 at 68.75%, and gm_8016B8D4 at 76.47%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching gm_16AE.c for completion of gm_80173EEC, fn_8016CA68, and gm_8016B8D4 after this partial improvement.
Search for further refinements to lbl_8046B6A0_t field names and StartMeleeRules x0_0/x3C/x40 semantics.
Look for later ifstock.h cleanup replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes added here with concrete signatures.
Compare future gm_16AE stock-share functions against fn_8016B918_inline and ifStock_802F7EFC usage.
Check whether lbTime_8000AF24's two-argument signature affected other callers or enabled additional lbtime matches.
